Arch-match — HandMotion/ObjMotion mirror 4RC (CrossBlock + AdaLNBlock, pre-norms, LayerScale, DiT AdaLN). Transfer 168/168.
Rot_err coord-frame fix: 4RC pose_enc is frame-0-relative, DexYCB GT absolute. Frame-0-relative RRA/RTA drops err 156° → 0.07°
Memory opt — activation checkpoint on 27 post-alt_start encoder blocks (−48% mem) + CPU scene GT (−131 MiB). bs=8 cl=4 now fits at ~44 GiB.
Ablation A (random) vs B (4RC-init) single-seq 3000 steps on old arch: B wins all metrics (hand 10.7 vs 12.6, obj 8.9 vs 13.8). Pre arch-match.
Path Y arm B′ (4RC-init on NEW arch, bs=8): final hand 10.74 / obj 9.93. Tied with old arch at step 3000 — arch win hidden by bs=8 confound or short horizon.

tasks_pending.summary: STAGE2_FULL — submit subject-01 multi-seq train at 426e0b3 + bs=8 cl=4 + --motion-init-from-4rc. Trainer CLI is ready (124f4fe); job not yet submitted — blocked on 3pm Asia/Shanghai rate-limit reset.
Re-probe bs=16 cl=4 on 8×H100 DDP — prior probe blocked on p1 quota while overfit jobs held 16 cards. bs=16 would 2× samples/step again (projected ~65 GiB).
Optional: rerun ablation arm A′ (random init, NEW arch, bs=4) to isolate arch effect from bs confound. Not blocking Stage 2.
ABLATION_MOTION_HEAD_INIT — the single-seq Path X/Y inversion (B′ ≤ B) is not a clean signal; the honest comparison point is Stage 2 multi-seq result. Leave for post-Stage-2 analysis.

discoveries.title: Handedness latent bug fix: 50% of DexYCB training was using wrong-sided GPS
discoveries.body: Pre-7cdd0f5 DexYCB16FrameClip silently dropped slot_index, so left-hand sequences (499/1000 seqs) trained on right-hand MANO eigvecs with the shared e_hand embedding. Fix: 6-file surgery (loader → encoder e_hand (2,1536) → query MLP → loss mask). Numerically proved left-hand MANO is exactly flip_x of right-hand (max diff 0.0, Laplacian identical) — reuses the right-hand GPS after x-flip, no need to recompute. All Stage-1 H1–H4 and earlier Stage-2 smokes predate the fix and are tainted.

discoveries.title: Forward pass is not the bottleneck — DataLoader is 62% of step wall-clock
discoveries.body: On 4×H100 DDP, cl=16 bs=1: dataloader 3.36 s / fwd 0.57 s / bwd 1.45 s / opt 10 ms / log 129 ms. Total wall 5.4 s but GPU busy only 2 s. Cause: num_workers hardcoded to 2, each worker doing 16× (JPEG decode + LANCZOS 518² + depth PNG + depthmap_to_absolute_camera_coords) per clip. Fix: --num-workers 8 --prefetch-factor 4 + persistent_workers (shipped in 5cc5faf).

discoveries.title: Pose rot_err 156° was a reference-frame bug, not a model error
discoveries.body: 4RC Arc.pose_enc returns cam-to-world RELATIVE TO FRAME 0. DexYCB GT extrinsic is absolute world-to-camera. Comparing pred vs GT without a frame-0 anchor leaked the world offset into the geodesic angle. Frame-0-relative RRA/RTA (pred·pred[0]⁻¹ vs GT·GT[0]⁻¹) drops rot_err from 156° to 0.002° on frame 0 and ≤0.07° on all 8 frames. The residual 0.06° drift is the pristine 4RC per-frame noise floor on a static camera. Prior publish pages that cited 156°/165° as a model error need retraction.

discoveries.title: Encoder gradient checkpointing cuts 48% of per-rank GPU memory
discoveries.body: Wrapping vit.process_attention in torch.utils.checkpoint for the 27 post-alt_start blocks (those carrying a trainable grad path) drops per-rank peak from 28 → 14.7 GiB at bs=1 cl=4. Blocks 0-12 skipped: requires_grad=False there, autograd already discards. Plus 131 MiB/rank saved by CPU-resident scene GT (never read in Stage 1 loss). Net: bs=8 cl=4 projected ~44 GiB (was OOM at 75.3). bs=16 cl=4 also plausible at ~65 GiB but unverified.

discoveries.title: 4RC → HOI3R motion-head transfer was 56/166 due to structural mismatch — now 168/168
discoveries.body: Pre-58532a2 HOI3R motion heads transferred only Q/K/V + MLP weights (56 of 166 params). AdaLN style, pre-norms, LayerScale, cross-attn normy were ALL different from 4RC MotionDecoder. 58532a2 rewrites HandMotion/ObjMotion to mirror 4RC exactly: CrossBlock + AdaLNBlock, pre-norms, LayerScale γ=0.01, DiT-style AdaLN (6×D: shift/scale/gate × {MSA, MLP}), normy(context) pre-norm. Rename fn collapsed from ~120 lines to 2 regex subs. 168/168 transferable (4 left random = delta_proj + uncert.proj, semantically head-specific).

discoveries.title: Ablation: 4RC-init beats random (as expected), but NEW arch did NOT beat OLD arch at 3000 steps
discoveries.body: Three-arm overfit on subject-01/20200709_141754. Arm A (random, old arch, bs=4): final hand 12.6 / obj 13.8, median-20 10.1 / 7.1. Arm B (4RC-init, old arch, bs=4): 10.7 / 8.9, median-20 9.2 / 6.0. Arm B′ (4RC-init, NEW arch, bs=8): 10.7 / 9.9, median-20 10.8 / 10.0. B > A holds (4RC-init wins). But B ≥ B′ on median-20 — arch-match did NOT cleanly outperform. Confounds: bs=8 vs bs=4, LayerScale γ=0.01 slow-start, or 3000 steps is just too short to see the asymptotic payoff. Verdict: use new arch for Stage 2 (paper-faithful + 168/168 transfer), but don't assume single-seq arch-match win; Stage 2 multi-seq is the real signal.

discoveries.title: Renderer: interactive Plotly + RGB-sampled scene + sparse FPS tracks is the best viz we've shipped
discoveries.body: v3 page at /updates/2026-04-23-renderer-v3.html: 4 views — scene-pred-only (no RGB bg), scene-bg hand+obj (no tracks), scene-bg hand+obj with tracks, Plotly interactive 3D with 16-frame slider. Scene uses per-point RGB sampled bilinearly from the input frame (via projected pixel coords). Hand/obj tracks are FPS-subsampled to 12 + 20 (from 128 + 256) for legibility. Plotly markers 1.5 for hand/obj, 1.8 for scene; scene alpha 0.85 so RGB shows. No viser, no pyrender — pure matplotlib + Plotly CDN.

module_state.state: FIXED @ 7cdd0f5. 2-slot e_hand (1536,)→(2,1536). L=x-flip of R numerically proven. Ready for bimanual HOT3D once select_active_hand_slot is relaxed.
ARCH-MATCHED @ 58532a2. HandMotionHead / ObjMotionHead mirror 4RC MotionDecoder (CrossBlock + AdaLNBlock). 168/168 transfer. bit-identical code modulo N=128/256.
Checkpointed @ b18a2d8. 27 post-alt_start blocks under torch.utils.checkpoint. Frozen (requires_grad=False on ViT-G).
cl=4, bs=8, num_workers=8, prefetch=4, --motion-init-from-4rc. Projected ~44 GiB/rank on 8×H100. 1.4 s/step est.
v3.2 @ 426e0b3 live at /updates/2026-04-23-renderer-v3.html. RGB scene colors, sparse FPS tracks, Plotly interactive.
camera_metrics frame-0-relative @ 3385712. Scene/pose rot_err now reads cleanly on DexYCB.
DexYCB subject-01 manifest (100 seqs) used throughout. HOT3D blocked on scene-depth + bimanual loss mask.

caveats: STAGE2_FULL (#T15) is ready to submit but not yet dispatched — blocked on 3pm Asia/Shanghai rate-limit reset. Trainer CLI is already on origin at 124f4fe.

New-arch ablation (B′) did NOT outperform old-arch ablation (B) at 3000 single-seq steps. Confounds: bs=8 vs bs=4, LayerScale γ=0.01 slow-start, or insufficient horizon. Treat Stage 2 multi-seq result as the real signal, not this single-seq inversion.
Arm A + B results (5cc5faf) are from the PRE arch-match architecture. A fresh A + B on the new arch (58532a2) would give cleaner arch-win data but costs 2× ACP hours and user deferred it.
Port 20161 is dead as of 2026-04-23 (container restart); use 21161. The primary may flip again on next restart — probe both at session start.
Git push from debug container TLS-flakes ~30-40% of tries. Use Mac bundle+push fallback (see feedback_debug_container_git_push.md) for multi-commit pushes.
expandable_segments:True is set in ACP env but NOT supported on the current image; allocator is default. Don't count on it to squeeze bs=N+1 on the next probe.
Publish pages before 2026-04-23 that cite pristine rot_err 156°/165° as a model error are wrong — the mismatch was reference-frame, not the model. Needs retraction note if anyone reads them.
